questions.q: The Nizam was the title of the ruler of which princely state?
questions.explanation: Nizam was the title of the rulers of the princely state of Hyderabad.

questions.q: Who founded the line of the Nizams of Hyderabad?
questions.explanation: The dynasty was founded by Asaf Jah I, a former Mughal governor.

questions.q: The founder of the Nizam's dynasty had originally served as a governor of which empire?
questions.explanation: Asaf Jah I was a Mughal governor who carved out an independent state as Mughal power waned.

questions.q: In which century did the Nizam's dynasty become effectively independent?
questions.explanation: Asaf Jah I established his autonomy in the Deccan in the early 18th century CE.

questions.q: The Nizam's domain lay in which broad region of India?
questions.explanation: The Nizam was styled lord of the Deccan, ruling a vast plateau domain.

questions.q: What was the name of the dynasty founded by Asaf Jah I?
questions.explanation: The line of Nizams is known as the Asaf Jahi dynasty.

questions.q: For roughly how long did the Asaf Jahi dynasty govern its Deccan domain?
questions.explanation: The Asaf Jahi Nizams ruled for over two centuries until the mid-20th century.

questions.q: The capital city of the Nizam's state shared its name with the state itself. What was it?
questions.explanation: The city of Hyderabad was the capital of the Nizam's princely state.

questions.q: The Nizam's state of Hyderabad became one of the largest of which type of state under British paramountcy?
questions.explanation: Hyderabad was the largest and richest of the princely states in British India.

questions.q: The fort city near Hyderabad famed for diamonds, earlier seat of the Qutb Shahis, was which place?
questions.explanation: Golconda, near Hyderabad, was renowned for its diamond trade and earlier ruled by the Qutb Shahis.

questions.q: What was the personal name and Mughal title of Asaf Jah I, founder of the Nizams?
questions.explanation: Asaf Jah I was Mir Qamar-ud-din Khan, also styled Chin Qilich Khan in Mughal service.

questions.q: Around which year is Asaf Jah I generally said to have established his independent rule in the Deccan?
questions.explanation: Asaf Jah I consolidated his autonomous power in the Deccan around 1724.

questions.q: Which battle of 1724 helped Asaf Jah I secure his control of the Deccan against a Mughal-appointed rival?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Shakar Kheda (Sakhar Kheda) in 1724 confirmed Asaf Jah I's grip on the Deccan.

questions.q: Which rising Indian power frequently pressed the Nizam for tribute (chauth) in the 18th century?
questions.explanation: The Marathas repeatedly clashed with and exacted tribute from the Nizam during the 18th century.

questions.q: The Nizam was an early Indian ruler to enter which kind of treaty arrangement with the British East India Company?
questions.explanation: The Nizam was among the first to accept the Subsidiary Alliance system under the British.

questions.q: Under British paramountcy the Nizam was honoured with which special form of address denoting his loyalty?
options: Faithful Ally of the British Government
questions.explanation: The Nizam bore the title 'Faithful Ally of the British Government' for his cooperation.

questions.q: The last ruling Nizam, famed for immense wealth, was which figure?
questions.explanation: Mir Osman Ali Khan, Asaf Jah VII, was the last ruling Nizam and reputedly among the world's richest men.

questions.q: The Nizam's state of Hyderabad was integrated into the Indian Union in which year?
questions.explanation: Hyderabad was annexed to India in 1948 through the military action known as Operation Polo.

questions.q: The 1948 Indian operation that absorbed Hyderabad was code-named what?
questions.explanation: Operation Polo was the police action that brought Hyderabad into the Indian Union in 1948.

questions.q: Which paramilitary force loyal to the last Nizam resisted integration into India?
questions.explanation: The Razakars, a militia, sought to keep Hyderabad independent and resisted accession to India.

questions.q: The fabled Jacob Diamond and vast jewel collection were associated with which Indian ruler's treasury?
questions.explanation: The Nizams accumulated extraordinary treasures, including the famous Jacob Diamond.

questions.q: How many rulers in total reigned in the Asaf Jahi line of Nizams?
questions.explanation: Seven Nizams of the Asaf Jahi dynasty ruled Hyderabad, from Asaf Jah I to Mir Osman Ali Khan.

questions.q: In the wars between the British, French, Marathas, and Mysore, the Nizam at one point allied with the British against whom?
questions.explanation: The Nizam joined the British and Marathas against Tipu Sultan in the Anglo-Mysore Wars.

questions.q: The Nizam's territory in the early period included which earlier Deccan-sultanate capitals as administrative centres?
questions.explanation: Aurangabad and Hyderabad served as key centres of the Nizam's Deccan administration.

questions.q: Which French-officered force did the Nizam maintain before disbanding it under British pressure around 1798?
options: A French-trained corps under officers like Raymond
questions.explanation: The Nizam kept a French-trained contingent (under officers such as Raymond) that he dismissed when entering the Subsidiary Alliance.

questions.q: The Berar region was ceded by the Nizam to British administration under an arrangement of which type?
options: An assignment/lease to defray subsidiary-force costs
A dowry for a royal marriage
questions.explanation: Berar was assigned to the British to cover the costs of the subsidiary force stationed in Hyderabad.

questions.q: Osmania University, founded by the last Nizam, was notable for initially using which language as its medium of instruction?
questions.explanation: Osmania University, established by Mir Osman Ali Khan in 1918, was the first to use Urdu as its medium of instruction.

questions.q: After 1948, the last Nizam was given which largely ceremonial position by the Indian government?
questions.explanation: Mir Osman Ali Khan was made Rajpramukh, a ceremonial constitutional head, of Hyderabad State after accession.

questions.q: The Nizam's dispute over funds deposited in a London bank account in 1948 became a long-running legal case against which country?
questions.explanation: The 'Hyderabad funds' dispute over money transferred to a London account pitted India and the Nizam's heirs against Pakistan.

questions.q: Architecturally, the Nizams are associated with which landmark Indo-Saracenic building in Hyderabad housing their court and offices?
questions.explanation: The Nizams are linked with the Chowmahalla and Falaknuma palaces of Hyderabad, seats of their court and grandeur.
